context: Tests whether the skill preserves the boundary between accessibility focus and keyboard/directional focus while still covering accessibility impacts of traversal, labels, and assistive technologies.

checklist.name: Accessibility focus
checklist.description: Keeps VoiceOver or accessibility focus restoration after modal dismissal in ios-accessibility scope.

checklist.name: Restoration API
checklist.description: Recommends `@AccessibilityFocusState` or an equivalent accessibility notification for accessibility focus restoration.

checklist.name: Modal behavior
checklist.description: Mentions custom modal accessibility behavior such as modal containment or escape dismissal.

checklist.name: Voice Control names
checklist.description: Keeps duplicate Voice Control names in ios-accessibility scope and recommends unique, speakable labels.

checklist.name: Input labels
checklist.description: Recommends `accessibilityInputLabels` where shorter spoken alternatives are useful.

checklist.name: Tab routing
checklist.description: Routes Tab traversal and custom-card keyboard focus implementation to focus-engine rather than solving it in accessibility guidance.

checklist.name: FocusState boundary
checklist.description: Routes `@FocusState`, `.focusable()`, and keyboard focus-state mechanics to focus-engine.

checklist.name: Directional boundary
checklist.description: Routes arrow-key movement, focus sections, scene focus, tvOS focus, and `UIFocusGuide` details to focus-engine.

checklist.name: Traversal impact
checklist.description: Still notes that accessibility element order and grouping affect VoiceOver, Switch Control, Voice Control, and keyboard accessibility outcomes.

checklist.name: Clear distinction
checklist.description: Explains the distinction between accessibility focus for assistive technologies and keyboard/directional focus in the scene.
